title: Iteration Protocols, Generators, and Lazy Evaluation · Pipeline Brief: One Shot Hazards
summary: Process records larger than memory while exposing malformed rows at a controlled boundary. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fp one shot hazards increment in a bounded-memory lazy ingestion pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent accidental exhaustion, duplicate consumption, and hidden ordering dependencies.
Measure memory and latency tradeoffs between eager and lazy transformation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict One Shot Hazards
steps.instruction:
theory case — A one-pass sensor iterator yields [6, 9, malformed, 14, 19]. Consume at most 8 records, report malformed input, and never restart or materialize the open-ended source.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the One Shot Hazards contract for the supplied data.
